level_3_metrics.metric_name: Number Of Corruption Or Bribery Convictions
user_friendly_explanation: Apple has a perfect score in this area because their reports do not mention any instances where the company or its main operations were found guilty of corruption or bribery. This suggests that Apple maintains high ethical standards and robust checks to prevent such misconduct. While the company regularly monitors its vast supply chain for various issues, no convictions for bribery or corruption against Apple itself were reported.
information: Apple's Board of Directors oversees the Chief Executive Officer and other senior management in the competent and ethical operation of Apple on a day-to-day basis and assures that the long-term interests of shareholders are being served. To satisfy the Board's duties, directors are expected to take a proactive, focused approach to their positions, and set standards to ensure that Apple is committed to business success through the maintenance of high standards of responsibility and ethics.

Apple's policies and standards, including our Apple Human Rights Policy and our Apple Supplier Code of Conduct and Supplier Responsibility Standards, outline the principles by which we operate and hold ourselves and our suppliers accountable... The Code's requirements cover labor and human rights, health and safety, the environment, ethics, and management practices.
In 2024, across more than 893 Code of Conduct assessments, we found no instances in our supply chain where people were forced to work.

level_3_metrics.metric_name: Gender Diversity At Board And Executive Level
user_friendly_explanation: Apple shows a strong commitment to gender diversity within its leadership. Half of its board members are women, demonstrating significant representation at the highest level. While specific numbers for all executive positions aren't fully disclosed, at least one prominent female executive is identified among the senior leadership. The company also has a clear policy to actively seek out diverse candidates, including those of different genders, for its board, indicating a structured approach to fostering diversity in its leadership ranks.
information: Apple's Board of Directors consists of 8 members, including 4 women (Wanda Austin, Andrea Jung, Monica Lozano, Susan L. Wagner) and 4 men (Arthur D. Levinson, Tim Cook, Alex Gorsky, Ronald D. Sugar). [Source: https://investor.apple.com/leadership-and-governance/default.aspx, Chunk: 280, 287]

Apple has 20 senior executive members, with Lisa Jackson identified as a female Vice President of Environment, Policy and Social Initiatives. [Source: Apple_CDP-Climate-Change-Questionnaire_2024.pdf, Page 26]

Apple's Nominating Committee considers diversity, including 'gender identity or expression', when evaluating potential nominees to the Board. The committee is committed to 'actively seeking out and will instruct any search firm it engages to identify, individuals who will contribute to the overall diversity of the Board to be included in the pool of candidates from which nominees to the Board are selected.' [Source: Apple_CDP-Climate-Change-Questionnaire_2024.pdf, Page 22]
score_explanation_french: Le score pour les femmes au conseil d'administration est de 33,33 points, calculé à partir de 4 femmes sur un total de 8 membres, ce qui représente 50 % de femmes au conseil, correspondant à la catégorie « 40 % ou plus de femmes au conseil d'administration ». Le score pour les femmes dans la direction exécutive est de 15 points, résultant de l'identification d'au moins 1 femme cadre sur 20 membres de la direction générale, ce qui représente un minimum de 5 % de femmes et correspond à la catégorie « 1 à 10 % de femmes dans la direction exécutive ». Le score pour la politique de diversité de genre du conseil/direction exécutive est de 25 points, basé sur une politique solide qui recherche activement des personnes diverses, y compris en matière de genre, pour les nominations au conseil et démontre des initiatives visant à améliorer la diversité du leadership, même sans objectifs numériques explicites, s'alignant sur les critères « Politique forte avec des objectifs et des échéanciers précis et mesurables. Preuve d'initiatives visant à améliorer la diversité du leadership et la divulgation ». Le score total est la somme de ces points : 33,33 + 15 + 25 = 73,33.
