score_explanation_french: L'entreprise fournit une ventilation complète des genres pour sa main-d'œuvre aux États-Unis, incluant le nombre total d'employés (101 536), le nombre d'employés masculins (66 261) et le nombre d'employés féminins (35 275). Étant donné que la somme des employés masculins et féminins est égale au total, les catégories « Autres » et « Non déclarés » sont implicitement nulles dans cet ensemble de données spécifiques aux États-Unis. De plus, l'entreprise fournit des pourcentages globaux par genre (64 % d'hommes, 35 % de femmes) et reconnaît explicitement que le genre n'est pas binaire, ce qui témoigne d'une prise de conscience des identités de genre non binaires, les 1 % restants dans les pourcentages globaux couvrant implicitement les catégories « Autres » ou « Non déclarés ». Cette divulgation complète de données numériques pour une partie importante de sa main-d'œuvre, ainsi que des pourcentages globaux et une déclaration explicite sur le genre non binaire, satisfait aux critères d'une ventilation complète des genres. Par conséquent, un score de 100 points est attribué.

user_friendly_explanation: Apple is committed to ensuring its employees and those in its supply chain work in safe conditions. It regularly checks if suppliers follow these rules through assessments (conducting 893 in 2024) and provides training to millions of employees (over 2.5 million in 2024) on their workplace rights and safety. From the strong foundation set by our Code, we go further-from helping suppliers transition to low-carbon electricity, to providing educational opportunities, to supporting suppliers in reducing waste. [Source: iPhone_16e_PER_Feb2025.pdf, Page 5]

score_explanation_french: L'entreprise présente des déclarations générales sur son engagement envers la santé et la sécurité des employés, ses pratiques de gestion EHS et ses normes strictes pour sa chaîne d'approvisionnement, y compris des évaluations et des formations régulières. user_friendly_explanation:
Apple is committed to ensuring a safe and healthy environment for everyone involved in making and using its products. They have a strong, publicly available plan that focuses on constantly improving safety, going beyond what laws require. This includes carefully controlling chemicals using a step-by-step process to reduce exposure, rigorously checking new materials for safety, and promoting the use of safer cleaning products with a special app.

Apple also makes sure its factories and repair services are safe by regularly inspecting machines, training workers extensively, and using only approved parts and tools for repairs, while warning about the dangers of unverified third-party components. They actively investigate any safety concerns, fix problems quickly, and involve employees through training and feedback programs, including mental wellness support. This comprehensive approach shows a deep commitment to protecting their workforce and customers.
information: Apple Inc. is committed to protecting the environment, health and safety (EHS) of our employees, customers, and contractors in the design, research, manufacture, distribution, and use of our products and services in global communities where we operate. Apple strives for continuous improvement in our EHS management systems and in the environmental quality of our products, processes, and services. We pursue continual improvement through the evaluation of our EHS performance by monitoring ongoing performance results through periodic management reviews, and committing to correct EHS nonconformities. We ensure that all employees are aware of their role and responsibility to fulfill and sustain Apple's EHS management systems and policy by providing training and tools in the user's primary language. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 122]

Apple's strict Supplier Code of Conduct and Supplier Responsibility Standards apply to all of our suppliers, regardless of where they are located, or what work they do for Apple. We evaluate our suppliers each year against more than 500 criteria - covering labor and human rights, health and safety, environmental protection, management practices, and ethics - to verify their compliance and identify areas for improvement. These assessments are conducted globally by internationally accredited, independent third-party auditing firms, and include management and employee interviews, extensive document reviews, and site walk-throughs. [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable]

We focus on limiting chemical exposure through the commonly used hierarchy of controls: Elimination, Substitution, Engineering controls, Administrative controls, Personal protective equipment (PPE). We prioritize elimination and substitution. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 59]

Our Regulated Substances Specification describes Apple's global requirements and restrictions on the use of certain chemical substances or materials in Apple products, accessories, packaging, and manufacturing processes. Apple's restrictions may go beyond regulatory requirements in order to protect human health and the environment. [Source: Apple_Regulated_Substances_Specification.pdf, Page 2]

In 2024, we performed toxicological assessments on more than 1800 new materials to proactively evaluate and eliminate potentially harmful substances from our products. We also use comprehensive assessments such as GreenScreen® to gauge the potential impact of chemicals on individuals' health and the environment. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 65]

In 2024, more than 1000 supplier facilities shared their chemical inventories as well as their storage and control information as part of the Chemical Safety Disclosure (CSD) program. We've identified more than 16,000 unique materials and chemicals used in the manufacturing process. All these efforts contribute to a safer work environment for people across our supply chain. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 62]

We've restricted the use of cleaners with known carcinogens, mutagens, reproductive toxicants, strong sensitizers, and persistent bio-accumulative toxins (including PFAS) from cleaners and degreasers used at our supplier final assembly sites. In 2024, we approved 67 additional safer cleaners for use in our supply chain, bringing the total to more than 200. For a fourth year, Apple has been recognized as a Safer Choice Partner of the Year by the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 65], [Source: supplier-responsibility]

In 2024, we launched CleanScreen, a cloud-based app designed to streamline the creation of safer formulations, in collaboration with ChemFORWARD. This app allows users to screen ingredients in their cleaners and degreasers and identify substitutions for chemicals of concern. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 66]

We’re regularly reviewing and strengthening our machine safety programs to help keep the people who operate manufacturing equipment safe on the job. We require suppliers to design safer equipment from the start and to conduct regular trainings on topics such as the use of safety devices, inspection basics, automation safety, and hazards associated with moving parts. In 2024, 800+ supplier sites enrolled in our enhanced machine safety training. [Source: supplier-responsibility]

Technicians in Apple's service and repair network are trained to perform battery replacements safely. They use tools custom-engineered to perform the repair, use only genuine Apple parts, and take care to avoid mishandling components. Self Service Repair provides customers access to the same genuine parts and tools, along with repair manuals to guide them on how to safely perform the replacement. [Source: Expanding_Access_to_Service_and_Repairs.pdf, Page 5]

Non-genuine parts and tools or poor workmanship during a repair have led to Apple devices being damaged or becoming unsafe. An independent study of third-party smartphone replacement batteries found that 88% caught fire or exploded in at least one test. We notify consumers when a third-party battery is installed so they are aware of the potential risk to their safety. [Source: Longevity_by_Design-June_2024-Apple.pdf, Pages 11, 12]

We promptly investigate any allegations we receive, with Apple experts typically onsite within 24 to 48 hours. If a violation of our standards is discovered, we require suppliers to promptly implement a plan to correct it, and to strengthen their policies and procedures to prevent the issue from reoccurring. We also require that suppliers provide remedy to their employees affected by these violations. [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable]

The 'Dormitories and Dining' section of the Code and Standards addresses water supply, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH), establishing clear requirements for toilets and bathrooms both at manufacturing facilities and dormitories. There are requirements for free availability of drinking water at all times and quality monitoring for safety. From 2018 to 2024, we have seen an over 60 percent reduction in findings related to topics like the management and operation of supplier wastewater systems, management of emergency preparedness, operation of stormwater management systems, and water quality monitoring and measurement systems. [Source: Apples_Water_Strategy.pdf, Page 20]

Since 2017, millions of supplier employees have benefited from training on essential topics such as nutrition, reproductive health, early disease detection, and mental health. In 2024, 54K+ participants in our mental wellbeing programs. [Source: supplier-responsibility]

All supplier employees are required to receive training on their workplace rights. Since 2008, more than 30 million people have been trained by our suppliers on their workplace rights. In 2024, we directly engaged over 1.3 million supplier employees on their workplace experience, including anonymous surveys, confidential interviews, and grievance channel awareness campaigns. [Source: apple-preventing-forced-labor-in-our-supply-chain]
In 2024, across more than 893 Code of Conduct assessments, we found no instances in our supply chain where people were forced to work. [Source: apple-preventing-forced-labor-in-our-supply-chain]
score_explanation_french:
Apple démontre une approche de pointe en matière de gestion de la santé et de la sécurité. L'entreprise a une politique EHS et un Code de Conduite des Fournisseurs complets et publiquement déclarés, s'engageant explicitement à protéger le bien-être de ses employés, clients et sous-traitants tout au long de sa chaîne de valeur. Cette politique est sujette à une amélioration continue par des révisions annuelles et vise à dépasser les exigences réglementaires.

Apple met en œuvre de vastes programmes de sécurité spécifiques pour identifier, prévenir et atténuer les dangers sur le lieu de travail. Cela inclut une approche robuste de « chimie plus intelligente » utilisant une hiérarchie de contrôles (élimination, substitution, ingénierie, administration, EPI), une Spécification des Substances Réglementées avec des restrictions chimiques strictes, des évaluations toxicologiques pour les nouveaux matériaux (plus de 1800 en 2024) et des divulgations de sécurité chimique de plus de 1000 fournisseurs. L'entreprise a approuvé plus de 200 nettoyants plus sûrs, a lancé l'application CleanScreen pour le développement de formulations plus sûres et aborde l'élimination des PFAS. Au-delà des produits chimiques, Apple a renforcé les programmes de sécurité des machines (plus de 800 sites en formation en 2024), mis en œuvre des protocoles rigoureux de sécurité des réparations (techniciens formés, pièces d'origine, outils personnalisés, avertissements concernant les risques liés aux tiers) et établi des exigences WASH pour ses installations et dortoirs.

L'évaluation des risques est intégrée à la gestion des produits chimiques par le biais des méthodologies GreenScreen® et ChemFORWARD®, et de la caractérisation toxicologique. L'entreprise assure la conformité par des audits d'usine réguliers et inopinés, des évaluations par des tiers (893 évaluations en 2024, dont 203 inopinées ; 100 évaluations RBA VAP) et des tests en laboratoire interne. Apple dispose d'un processus clair pour enquêter sur les allégations (sur site dans les 24 à 48 heures) et exige des actions correctives immédiates et des réparations pour les travailleurs affectés, favorisant l'amélioration continue. Cela est attesté par une réduction de 60 % des constatations liées à la gestion de l'eau de 2018 à 2024 et par l'amélioration des scores d'évaluation sur les sites des fournisseurs.

L'implication des employés est une pierre angulaire de la stratégie d'Apple, avec plus de 30 millions d'employés de fournisseurs formés aux droits du travail depuis 2008, et 1,3 million directement engagés en 2024 par le biais d'enquêtes et d'entretiens. L'entreprise propose également de vastes programmes de santé et de bien-être, y compris un soutien à la santé mentale (plus de 54 000 participants en 2024). Le suivi proactif des heures de travail pour 1,4 million de travailleurs dans plus de 400 installations souligne davantage un système de gestion complet et actif.
score_explanation_english:
Apple demonstrates a leading approach to Health and Safety Management. The company has a publicly stated comprehensive EHS Policy and Supplier Code of Conduct, explicitly committing to protecting the well-being of its employees, customers, and contractors across its value chain. This policy is subject to continuous improvement through annual reviews and aims to exceed regulatory requirements.

Apple implements extensive specific safety programs to identify, prevent, and mitigate workplace hazards. This includes a robust 'Smarter Chemistry' approach utilizing a hierarchy of controls (elimination, substitution, engineering, administrative, PPE), a Regulated Substances Specification with strict chemical restrictions, toxicological assessments for new materials (1800+ in 2024), and chemical safety disclosures from over 1000 suppliers. The company has approved over 200 safer cleaners, launched the CleanScreen app for safer formulation development, and addresses PFAS phase-out. Beyond chemicals, Apple has strengthened machine safety programs (800+ sites in training in 2024), implemented rigorous repair safety protocols (trained technicians, genuine parts, custom tools, warnings about third-party risks), and established WASH requirements for its facilities and dormitories.

Risk assessment is integrated into chemical management through GreenScreen® and ChemFORWARD® methodologies, and toxicological profiling. The company ensures compliance through regular and unannounced factory audits, third-party assessments (893 assessments in 2024, including 203 unannounced; 100 RBA VAP assessments), and internal lab testing. Apple has a clear process for investigating allegations (onsite within 24-48 hours) and requires immediate corrective actions and remedy for affected workers, fostering continuous improvement. This is evidenced by a 60% reduction in water management-related findings from 2018-2024 and improved assessment scores at supplier sites.

Employee involvement is a cornerstone of Apple's strategy, with over 30 million supplier employees trained on workplace rights since 2008, and 1.3 million directly engaged in 2024 through surveys and interviews. The company also offers extensive health and wellness programs, including mental well-being support (54K+ participants in 2024). The proactive monitoring of working hours for 1.4 million workers across 400+ facilities further highlights a comprehensive and active management system.

score_explanation_french: Apple déclare explicitement avoir "atteint et maintenu l'équité salariale entre les sexes à l'échelle mondiale" depuis 2017. Cette affirmation de parité est appuyée par leur pratique annuelle de partenariat avec un expert tiers indépendant pour examiner la rémunération totale et apporter les ajustements nécessaires pour garantir l'équité salariale. Cela démontre une preuve claire de parité ou de quasi-parité salariale entre les sexes, ainsi que des politiques transparentes et des examens réguliers pour la maintenir, ce qui correspond aux critères de notation les plus élevés.

This strong claim of parity is supported by their annual practice of partnering with an independent third-party expert to examine total compensation and make necessary adjustments to ensure pay equity. This demonstrates clear evidence of gender pay parity, along with transparent policies and regular reviews to maintain it, aligning with the highest scoring criteria.

user_friendly_explanation: Apple is doing an excellent job in providing training to its employees and those in its supply chain. They offer a wide range of programs, from basic workplace rights to specialized skills like coding and advanced manufacturing, as well as health and safety training. They've trained millions of people over the years, and regularly report how many individuals are participating in these different programs, showing a strong commitment to improving skills and well-being across their operations. This means they are actively investing in their workforce's development and safety.
information: Apple supports supplier employees with various training and education programs through the Apple Supplier Employee Development Fund, a $50 million commitment. Since its launch, over 5.7 million training and education sessions have been delivered, reaching more than 8 million people since 2008. [Source: supplier-responsibility, Page 208]
In 2024, over 2.5 million supplier employees were trained on their workplace rights. [Source: supplier-responsibility, Page 208]

Since 2008, more than 30 million people have been trained by Apple's suppliers on their workplace rights. This includes mandatory pre-departure, onboarding, and regular refresher training for Foreign Contract Workers. [Source: apple-preventing-forced-labor-in-our-supply-chain, Page 1]

Training opportunities for supplier employees include coding (Swift coding program with over 340,000 participants since 2017), smart manufacturing fundamentals, language learning, health training (reaching over 5.8 million people since 2017, with more than 54,000 participants in mental wellbeing programs in 2024), and leadership development. [Source: supplier-responsibility, Page 208]

Specialized training programs include machine safety (over 800 supplier sites enrolled in enhanced machine safety training in 2024 and over 1,700 sites in safety training since 2022), chemical safety, sustainability basics, zero waste roadmap, and waste management best practices. [Source: supplier-responsibility, Page 208]

In 2024, more than 480 participants attended trainings on VOC regulations, helping validate over 3,900 materials for low-VOC compliance. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 65]
score_explanation_french: Apple fait preuve de pratiques exemplaires en matière de formation et de développement des employés. L'entreprise fournit des informations vastes et complètes sur diverses initiatives de formation, décrivant clairement les types de formation offerts (par exemple, droits du travail, codage, santé, sécurité, chimie plus intelligente, zéro déchet) et identifiant explicitement les groupes d'employés ciblés, principalement les employés des fournisseurs et les travailleurs étrangers sous contrat. De manière cruciale, Apple fournit des données quantitatives significatives concernant la participation, y compris des millions de personnes formées sur leurs droits au travail depuis 2008 (plus de 30 millions), des millions atteintes par des programmes de santé et de bien-être, et des chiffres spécifiques pour les programmes de codage et de bien-être mental au cours des dernières années (par exemple, plus de 2,5 millions formés sur les droits au travail en 2024, plus de 5,7 millions de sessions de formation et d'éducation dispensées). Bien que les heures de formation moyennes par employé ne soient pas directement indiquées, le volume considérable de participants et de sessions rapportés, combiné à la portée et à l'objectif détaillés des programmes, correspond aux critères « Chef de file » pour la divulgation de données quantitatives et la communication complète.
score_explanation_english: Apple demonstrates leading practices in employee training and development. The company provides extensive and comprehensive disclosures of various training initiatives, clearly outlining the types of training offered (e.g., workplace rights, coding, health, safety, smarter chemistry, zero waste) and explicitly identifying target employee groups, primarily supplier employees and foreign contract workers. Crucially, Apple provides significant quantitative data regarding participation, including millions of people trained on workplace rights since 2008 (30M+), millions reached by health and wellness programs, and specific figures for coding and mental wellbeing programs in recent years (e.g., 2.5M+ trained on workplace rights in 2024, 5.7M+ training and education sessions delivered). While average training hours per employee are not directly stated, the sheer volume of participants and sessions reported, combined with the detailed scope and purpose of the programs, aligns with the 'Leading' criteria for quantitative data disclosure and comprehensive reporting.

user_friendly_explanation: Apple has a very strong commitment to human rights, with clear rules for how its own operations and all its suppliers must treat people fairly. They follow global best practices from organizations like the UN and the International Labour Organization. Apple goes to great lengths to prevent issues like forced labor, ensuring workers are treated with respect and even helping to repay fees unfairly charged to them. They regularly check on their suppliers, including surprise visits, and listen to feedback from millions of workers. If problems arise, they act quickly to fix them and ensure affected workers receive help. This comprehensive and active approach shows Apple is deeply committed to upholding human rights throughout its business.
information: Apple has a formal "Human Rights Policy, The Apple Supplier Code of Conduct ('Code'), and Supplier Responsibility Standards ('Standards')" that apply universally to all suppliers in over 50 countries and regions, including sub-tier suppliers and labor agencies [Source: apple-preventing-forced-labor-in-our-supply-chain, Page 1; apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable, Page 1; Apple_CDP-Climate-Change-Questionnaire_2024.pdf, Page 11, 29].

Apple’s requirements and due diligence practices are aligned with internationally recognized human rights frameworks, including the UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights (UNGPs), the OECD Guidelines for Multinational Enterprises, and the ILO Core Conventions (International Labour Organization) [Source: apple-preventing-forced-labor-in-our-supply-chain, Page 1; apple-how-we-source-materials-responsibly, Page 1; Apple_CDP-Climate-Change-Questionnaire_2024.pdf, Page 29].

Key human rights risks are covered, including: forced labor (strictly prohibiting recruitment fees, with $34.5 million repaid to over 37,700 employees in 2024) [Source: apple-preventing-forced-labor-in-our-supply-chain, Page 1], discrimination (through the Racial Equity and Justice Initiative) [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2024.pdf, Page 111], and accident prevention/health and safety (covered in the Supplier Code of Conduct and assessed across 500 criteria) [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable, Page 1; Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2024.pdf, Page 97; Expanding_Access_to_Service_and_Repairs.pdf, Page 6]. Water access, sanitation, and hygiene (WASH) are also addressed in the "Dormitories and Dining" section of the Code and Standards [Source: Apples_Water_Strategy.pdf, Page 20].

Human rights due diligence is systematic, data-driven, and ongoing. It includes: pre-award risk checks for new suppliers (125 facilities assessed in 2024) [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable, Page 1], weekly monitoring of worker sentiment and working hours for 1.4 million workers across 400+ facilities [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable, Page 1], over 9,000 worker interviews at 58 supplier facilities in 2024 [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable, Page 1], 893 assessments (including 203 unannounced visits) conducted in 2024 [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable, Page 1], mandatory RBA Validated Assessment Program (VAP) assessments (100 completed in 2024) [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable, Page 1], mapping of over 2,800 labor agencies [Source: apple-preventing-forced-labor-in-our-supply-chain, Page 1], and a Responsible Labor Recruitment Due Diligence Toolkit [Source: apple-preventing-forced-labor-in-our-supply-chain, Page 1].

Apple actively monitors, reports, and remedies violations through structured processes. Grievance channels, including internal and third-party hotlines, are available to workers [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable, Page 1; apple-preventing-forced-labor-in-our-supply-chain, Page 1]. Allegations are investigated promptly (within 24-48 hours) [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable, Page 1]. Violations result in corrective action plans, probation, and potential termination of business, with required remedy for affected workers [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable, Page 1; apple-preventing-forced-labor-in-our-supply-chain, Page 1].

The company engages with a wide range of stakeholders, including supplier employees, civil society organizations, academic experts, and partners like the ILO, IOM, RBA, and the Fund for Global Human Rights [Source: apple-preventing-forced-labor-in-our-supply-chain, Page 1; apple-how-we-source-materials-responsibly, Page 1; Apple_CDP-Climate-Change-Questionnaire_2024.pdf, Page 29]. Transparent reporting includes annual progress reports and lists of smelters/refiners [Source: apple-preventing-forced-labor-in-our-supply-chain, Page 1].
score_explanation_french: Apple obtient un score de 100 pour ses politiques et processus en matière de droits humains. L'entreprise déclare explicitement disposer d'une politique globale en matière de droits humains, comprenant une politique des droits humains, un code de conduite des fournisseurs et des normes de responsabilité des fournisseurs. Ces politiques sont appliquées universellement à ses propres opérations et à l'ensemble de sa chaîne d'approvisionnement, y compris les sous-traitants. Les politiques sont explicitement alignées sur les principaux cadres internationaux tels que les Principes directeurs des Nations Unies relatifs aux entreprises et aux droits de l'homme (UNGPs), les Lignes directrices de l'OCDE et les Conventions fondamentales de l'OIT. Apple couvre un large éventail de risques clés en matière de droits humains, y compris des processus détaillés pour le travail forcé (avec des résultats mesurables pour le remboursement des frais de recrutement), la discrimination, la prévention des accidents/santé et sécurité, et l'accès à l'eau, l'assainissement et l'hygiène (WASH). L'entreprise met en œuvre un processus de diligence raisonnable robuste et systématique, axé sur les données et continu, impliquant des vérifications des risques avant l'attribution des contrats, une surveillance étendue (sentiment hebdomadaire des travailleurs et heures de travail), des évaluations fréquentes (annoncées et inopinées, audits tiers comme le RBA VAP) et la cartographie de la chaîne d'approvisionnement. Apple fait preuve de transparence dans ses rapports, s'engage activement auprès de nombreuses parties prenantes et organisations expertes, et dispose de mécanismes bien définis pour traiter les violations, garantissant une enquête rapide, des mesures correctives et une responsabilisation, avec des conséquences claires en cas de non-conformité. Le niveau de détail étendu et l'approche intégrée pour tous les critères justifient le score le plus élevé.
score_explanation_english: Apple scores 100 for its Human Rights Policies And Processes. The company explicitly states having a comprehensive human rights policy, including a Human Rights Policy, Supplier Code of Conduct, and Supplier Responsibility Standards. These policies are universally applied across its own operations and entire supply chain, including sub-tiers. The policies are explicitly aligned with leading international frameworks such as the UNGPs, OECD Guidelines, and ILO Core Conventions. Apple covers a broad range of key human rights risks, including detailed processes for forced labor (with measurable outcomes for recruitment fee repayment), discrimination, accident prevention/health and safety, and WASH. The company implements a robust and systematic due diligence process that is data-driven and ongoing, involving pre-award risk checks, extensive monitoring (weekly worker sentiment and working hours), frequent assessments (announced and unannounced, third-party audits like RBA VAP), and supply chain mapping. Apple demonstrates transparent reporting, actively engages with numerous stakeholders and expert organizations, and has well-defined mechanisms for addressing violations, ensuring prompt investigation, remediation, and accountability, with clear consequences for non-compliance. The extensive detail and integrated approach across all criteria warrant the highest score.

user_friendly_explanation:
Apple has a strong commitment to human rights, with no confirmed instances of forced labor found in its supply chain during 2024. The company also upholds policies promoting equal opportunity and fair pay for its own employees and actively monitors labor conditions across its vast network of suppliers. This comprehensive approach ensures that human rights are respected throughout its operations and extended value chain. 

*   No forced labor incidents found in the supply chain.
*   Strong policies for equal opportunity and fair pay for employees.
*   Extensive monitoring of labor and human rights across the supply chain.
information: In 2024, across more than 893 Code of Conduct assessments, Apple found no instances in its supply chain where people were forced to work.
Apple does not tolerate forced labor, and forced labor in any form is a Core Violation of their requirements.

Apple is an equal opportunity employer that seeks to promote equal opportunities for any employee or applicant without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, Veteran status, or other legally protected characteristics.
Since 2017, Apple has achieved and maintained gender pay equity globally, and in the United States, they have also achieved pay equity with respect to race, ethnicity, and gender.
Apple strives to protect the right of everyone to work in a safe and healthy environment where they’re treated with dignity and respect, including suppliers and their employees.
Apple requires its suppliers to educate their employees on their workplace rights, including how to share feedback directly if those rights aren’t being respected.

Apple reviews worker sentiment weekly for key supplier sites to identify emerging labor and human rights risks, and addresses any worker concerns through various channels, including anonymous surveys, confidential interviews, and grievance hotlines. In 2024, over 1.3 million supplier employees were engaged directly.

Apple restricts the workweek for supplier employees to 60 hours (including voluntary overtime) with at least one day off every seven days. In 2024, weekly data on working hours for an average of 1.4 million workers across more than 400 facilities was received to confirm compliance.
score_explanation_french: L'entreprise déclare explicitement qu'aucun cas de travail forcé n'a été constaté dans sa chaîne d'approvisionnement, suite à plus de 893 évaluations du Code de Conduite réalisées en 2024. Le travail forcé est une grave violation des droits humains, et les procédures établies par l'entreprise (évaluations) n'ont pas identifié de telle non-conformité. Pour ses propres effectifs, l'entreprise met en avant des politiques solides en matière d'égalité des chances, d'équité salariale et de droits du travail. Aucun incident confirmé de violation des droits humains (par exemple, actions en justice ou plaintes formelles) n'est signalé au sein de ses propres opérations ou impliquant d'autres parties prenantes (communautés affectées, consommateurs, utilisateurs finaux), tel que défini par la métrique. Bien que des 'améliorations apportées aux lieux de travail des fournisseurs' soient mentionnées, celles-ci ne sont pas classées comme des violations confirmées des droits humains dans le texte fourni. Par conséquent, sur la base de l'absence explicite de violations graves et confirmées des droits humains dans la chaîne de valeur et de l'absence de signalement d'incidents confirmés au sein de ses propres effectifs ou dans d'autres domaines, l'entreprise répond aux critères d'un score de 100.
score_explanation_english: The company explicitly states that across more than 893 Code of Conduct assessments in 2024, no instances of forced labor were found in its supply chain. Forced labor is a severe human rights issue, and the company's established procedures (assessments) did not identify any such non-compliance. For its own workforce, the company highlights strong policies on equal opportunity, pay equity, and labor rights, and there are no reported confirmed incidents of human rights violations (e.g., legal actions or formal complaints) within the undertaking's own operations or involving other stakeholders (affected communities, consumers, end-users) as defined by the metric. While 'improvements made to supplier workplaces' are mentioned, these are not categorized as confirmed human rights violations in the provided text. Therefore, based on the explicit absence of severe confirmed human rights violations in the value chain and the lack of reported confirmed incidents in its own workforce or other areas, the company meets the criteria for a score of 100.

user_friendly_explanation: Apple shows a very strong commitment to making sure its products are safe for everyone. They rigorously test their products for reliability, environmental impact, and chemical safety, even checking how materials interact with skin. They also follow all necessary safety rules, like those for restricting harmful chemicals, and often go above and beyond what's legally required. For example, they're actively working to remove all PFAS chemicals from their products and have programs to ensure their suppliers use safer cleaning materials. When safety issues or recalls happen, Apple has clear programs to fix them and actively encourages feedback from workers in its supply chain to quickly address any concerns. This comprehensive approach means they're always working to improve product safety for their customers and the environment.
information: Apple is committed to protecting the environment, health and safety (EHS) of its employees, customers, and contractors in the design, research, manufacture, distribution, and use of its products and services. The company strives for continuous improvement in its EHS management systems and the environmental quality of its products and processes. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 122]
Apple creates products that are safe for their intended use and manufactured in alignment with strict environmental standards. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 122]

The company conducts comprehensive product testing in its own Reliability and Environmental Testing Labs, including rigorous testing methods that simulate customer experiences. [Source: iPhone_16_and_iPhone_16_Plus_PER_Sept2024.pdf, Page 7; Source: MacBook_Pro_14-inch_M5_PER_Oct2025.pdf, Page 3]

Toxicological assessments are performed on materials, and biocompatibility testing extends to individual materials, modules, and entire products, especially for those with prolonged skin contact. In 2024, over 1800 new materials underwent toxicological assessments to proactively eliminate harmful substances. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 61; Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 65]

Apple provides cloud-based diagnostic systems to repair technicians, allowing them to diagnose issues without requiring customer passwords, thus preserving security and privacy. [Source: Longevity_by_Design-June_2024-Apple.pdf, Page 11]

Critical safety protections are in place for lasers in iPhone and iPad models, with multiple hardware safeguards to ensure compliance with safety standards and prevent emissions exceeding limits. [Source: Longevity_by_Design-June_2024-Apple.pdf, Page 11]

All Apple parts are engineered and tested to meet high safety and quality standards, equivalent to parts used in new products. Repair tools are also custom-engineered, tested, and verified for performance, functionality, and safety. [Source: Expanding_Access_to_Service_and_Repairs.pdf, Page 4; Source: Expanding_Access_to_Service_and_Repairs.pdf, Page 5]

The Regulated Substances Specification (RSS) outlines Apple's global requirements and restrictions on chemical substances in products, accessories, packaging, and manufacturing processes, often exceeding regulatory requirements. [Source: Apple_Regulated_Substances_Specification.pdf, Page 2]

Apple explicitly states compliance with various regulations, including the European Restriction of Hazardous Substances (RoHS) Directive (2011/65/EU and amendments) and the Waste Electrical and Electronic Equipment (WEEE) Directive. [Source: iPad_mini_PER_Oct2024.pdf, Page 11; Source: answers]

The company has a proactive approach to restricting hazardous substances, including a commitment to eliminating Per- and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances (PFAS) from products and manufacturing processes. PFOA and PFOS were restricted in 2010 and 2013, respectively, ahead of global requirements. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 70; Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2024.pdf, Page 64]

The RSS has been updated to include new requirements for substitution with safer alternatives and specific testing for heavy metals in dyes. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 64]

Apple has approved over 200 safer cleaners for use in its supply chain, with 67 new approvals in 2024, and actively promotes their use across the industry, including launching the CleanScreen app in collaboration with ChemFORWARD. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 65; Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 66]

Specific product safety issues are addressed through Apple Service Programs, such as the 'Mac mini Service Program for No Power Issue,' 'iPhone 14 Plus Service Program for Rear Camera Issue,' and '15-inch MacBook Pro Battery Recall Program.' [Source: support.apple.com?cid=gn-ols-home-hp-tab]

Apple promptly investigates any allegations of violations in its supply chain, with experts typically onsite within 24 to 48 hours, and requires suppliers to implement corrective actions and provide remedy to affected employees. [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable]

The company actively monitors worker sentiment at priority supplier sites weekly, collecting feedback via social media, internal grievance channels, third-party hotlines, and anonymous surveys. [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable]
score_explanation_french: L'entreprise obtient un score de 8 sur 8 pour la sécurité des produits. Elle fait preuve d'une approche exhaustive en matière de tests de produits, incluant des évaluations de fiabilité, environnementales, toxicologiques et de biocompatibilité, et gère ses propres laboratoires de tests de fiabilité et environnementaux. L'entreprise déclare explicitement sa conformité aux normes réglementaires pertinentes telles que RoHS, DEEE et REACH, et respecte sa propre spécification des substances réglementées qui dépasse souvent les exigences légales. Apple a une politique proactive de restriction et d'élimination des substances dangereuses, fournissant des exemples spécifiques comme les BFR, le PVC, l'arsenic, le mercure, et s'est engagée à éliminer les PFAS, soutenue par des initiatives comme l'application CleanScreen et des collaborations industrielles. L'entreprise gère activement les incidents liés à la sécurité des produits, avec des programmes de service et de rappel divulgués pour des produits spécifiques. Elle détaille également des mécanismes robustes pour enquêter et résoudre les préoccupations de sécurité et de qualité liées aux produits, y compris la responsabilité des fournisseurs et les systèmes de retour d'information des employés.
score_explanation_english: The company scores 8 out of 8 for Product Safety. It demonstrates comprehensive product testing, including reliability, environmental, toxicological, and biocompatibility assessments, and maintains its own Reliability and Environmental Testing Labs. The company explicitly states compliance with relevant regulatory standards such as RoHS, WEEE, and REACH, and adheres to its own Regulated Substances Specification which often exceeds legal requirements. Apple has a proactive policy for hazardous substance restriction and elimination, providing specific examples like BFRs, PVC, arsenic, mercury, and a commitment to phasing out PFAS, supported by initiatives like the CleanScreen app and industry collaborations. The company actively manages product safety incidents, with disclosed service programs and recall programs for specific products. It also details robust mechanisms for investigating and resolving product-related safety and quality concerns, including supplier accountability and employee feedback systems.

user_friendly_explanation: Apple goes to great lengths to listen to its workers, particularly in its supply chain. They gather feedback through various methods like anonymous surveys, private interviews, and even social media, checking worker feelings every week at many key locations. In 2024 alone, they spoke directly with over a million supplier employees. More importantly, they don't just listen; they take action. Based on this feedback, they made over 7,500 improvements in workplaces in 2024 and even follow up with tens of thousands of employees to ensure no one faces negative consequences for speaking up. This shows a strong commitment to continuously improving working conditions based on what their employees say.
information: Apple collects worker feedback via anonymous surveys, confidential interviews, social media platforms, internal grievance channels, and third-party hotlines. [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable]
In 2024, Apple conducted over 9,000 worker interviews at 58 supplier facilities as they ramped up production. [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable]
Apple reviews worker sentiment on a weekly basis for more than 50 priority supplier sites. [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable]

In 2024, Apple directly engaged with more than 1.3 million supplier employees on their workplace experience, including anonymous surveys, confidential interviews, and grievance channel awareness campaigns. [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable; Source: apple-preventing-forced-labor-in-our-supply-chain; Source: supplier-responsibility]

As part of its supplier assessment process, tens of thousands of confidential interviews are conducted with supplier employees about their workplace experiences. [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable]

Apple made more than 7,500 improvements to supplier workplaces in 2024 based on employee feedback. [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable; Source: diversity; Source: supplier-responsibility]

More than 665,000 people at 42 supplier sites were reached by hotline awareness campaigns, which provide information on how to raise workplace concerns. [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable; Source: diversity; Source: supplier-responsibility]

More than 37,000 supplier employees were contacted after interviews to ensure that they did not experience retaliation. [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable; Source: diversity; Source: supplier-responsibility]

Apple promptly investigates any allegations received, with Apple experts typically onsite within 24 to 48 hours. [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable; Source: apple-preventing-forced-labor-in-our-supply-chain; Source: diversity; Source: supplier-responsibility]

If a violation is discovered, suppliers are required to promptly implement a Corrective Action Plan (CAP) and provide remedy to affected employees. [Source: apple-how-we-hold-suppliers-accountable; Source: apple-preventing-forced-labor-in-our-supply-chain]
Apple provides a feedback form on its website for anonymous concerns. [Source: diversity; Source: supplier-responsibility]
score_explanation_french: L'entreprise engage activement ses employés via de multiples mécanismes de rétroaction, y compris des enquêtes anonymes, des entretiens confidentiels, des plateformes de médias sociaux, des canaux de réclamation internes et des lignes d'assistance tierces. La fréquence de l'engagement est décrite avec des examens hebdomadaires du sentiment des travailleurs sur plus de 50 sites fournisseurs prioritaires. L'étendue de l'engagement est vaste, avec plus de 9 000 entretiens avec des travailleurs menés dans 58 installations de fournisseurs et un engagement direct avec plus de 1,3 million d'employés de fournisseurs en 2024. De manière cruciale, l'entreprise divulgue des résultats clairs et des actions prises sur la base de ces commentaires, signalant plus de 7 500 améliorations apportées aux lieux de travail des fournisseurs en 2024 directement grâce aux commentaires des employés. De plus, des actions de suivi comme le contact avec plus de 37 000 employés pour prévenir les représailles et l'enquête rapide sur les allégations (dans les 24 à 48 heures) démontrent une approche complète et transparente pour agir sur les commentaires des employés, remplissant ainsi tous les critères pour un score « Leading ».
score_explanation_english: The company actively engages employees through multiple feedback mechanisms, including anonymous surveys, confidential interviews, social media platforms, internal grievance channels, and third-party hotlines. The frequency of engagement is described with weekly reviews of worker sentiment at over 50 priority supplier sites. The scope of engagement is extensive, with over 9,000 worker interviews conducted at 58 supplier facilities and direct engagement with more than 1.3 million supplier employees in 2024. Crucially, the company provides clear outcomes and actions taken based on this feedback, reporting over 7,500 improvements made to supplier workplaces in 2024 as a direct result of employee input. Furthermore, follow-up actions like contacting over 37,000 employees to prevent retaliation and prompt investigation of allegations (within 24-48 hours) demonstrate a comprehensive and transparent approach to acting on employee input, fulfilling all criteria for a 'Leading' score.

user_friendly_explanation: Apple has provided a very detailed and clear picture of its energy use for its own operations in fiscal year 2024. They've told us the exact total amount of energy consumed and broken it down by whether it's electricity or different types of fuels. They also clearly show how much of this energy comes from renewable sources like wind and solar, versus non-renewable sources like natural gas and diesel. This comprehensive information covers all their main facilities worldwide, helping us understand their energy footprint transparently.
information: For the fiscal year 2024, Apple's corporate facilities and colocation facilities consumed a total of 4,896,425.65 MWh of energy. This figure is broken down into electricity and various fuel types.

Electricity consumption for corporate facilities totaled 3,777,000 MWh, all of which was sourced from renewable energy. Colocation facilities consumed 527,655.65 MWh of electricity, also entirely from renewable sources. Therefore, total operational electricity consumption was 4,304,655.65 MWh, with 100% being renewable.

Fuel consumption for corporate facilities amounted to 591,770 MWh. This includes 183,330 MWh from renewable biogas and 408,440 MWh from non-renewable sources such as natural gas (307,390 MWh), propane liquid (1,760 MWh), gasoline (45,140 MWh), and diesel (54,150 MWh, which is 36,150 MWh for other diesel and 18,000 MWh for mobile combustion diesel).
The organizational boundary for this data includes Apple's corporate offices, retail stores, data centers, and colocation facilities worldwide for the fiscal year 2024.

In fiscal year 2024, suppliers sourced nearly 18 gigawatts of renewable energy, avoiding nearly 22 million metric tons of greenhouse gas emissions. Renewable electricity used by the supply chain totaled 31,300,000 MWh.
score_explanation_french: L'entreprise fournit une divulgation complète et de haute qualité de sa consommation totale d'énergie. La consommation totale d'énergie pour les opérations de l'entreprise (y compris les installations de colocation) pour l'exercice fiscal 2024 est divulguée quantitativement à 4 896 425,65 MWh. Une ventilation détaillée par type d'énergie (électricité et divers carburants) est fournie. La divulgation distingue clairement les sources renouvelables et non renouvelables pour l'électricité et les carburants. Enfin, les périmètres organisationnels (installations d'entreprise et de colocation dans le monde entier) et la période (exercice fiscal 2024) sont explicitement indiqués. Tous les critères pour un score de 100 sont remplis.
score_explanation_english: The company provides a full, high-quality disclosure of its total energy consumption. The total energy consumption for corporate operations (including colocation facilities) for fiscal year 2024 is quantitatively disclosed as 4,896,425.65 MWh. A detailed breakdown by energy type (electricity and various fuels) is provided. The disclosure clearly distinguishes between renewable and non-renewable sources for both electricity and fuels. Finally, the organizational boundaries (corporate and colocation facilities worldwide) and the time period (fiscal year 2024) are explicitly stated. All criteria for a score of 100 are met.

user_friendly_explanation: Apple has fully reported its direct greenhouse gas emissions, known as Scope 1 emissions, which come from sources it owns or controls. The company provides a precise total amount for these emissions, measured in units of carbon dioxide equivalent, and shows how these numbers have changed over several years. Apple also breaks down these emissions by the types of gases released and the different parts of its business where they occur. The methods used to calculate these emissions are clearly explained and follow international guidelines, and an independent expert has checked and confirmed the accuracy of 100% of these reported emissions.
information: Apple's carbon footprint includes direct scope 1 emissions. For fiscal year 2024, corporate Scope 1 emissions totaled 55,200 metric tons CO2e, including 37,400 metric tons from natural gas, diesel, and propane, 15,400 metric tons from fleet vehicles, and 2,400 metric tons from other emissions (R&D processes and refrigerant leaks). For fiscal year 2023, corporate Scope 1 emissions were 55,200 metric tons CO2e, with 35,300 metric tons from natural gas, diesel, and propane, 17,000 metric tons from fleet vehicles, and 2,900 metric tons from other emissions. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 82]

Apple follows the GHG Protocol Corporate Standard for quantifying and reporting direct greenhouse gas emissions. Actual consumption data is sourced from utility bills, invoices, meters, and vendor reports. Where actual data is unavailable, estimation factors per square foot per year are used. Emission factors from the U.S. Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and the UK Department for Environment, Food & Rural Affairs (DEFRA) are applied and updated annually. [Source: Apple_CDP-Climate-Change-Questionnaire_2024.pdf, Page 90]

Scope 1 GHG Emissions are broken down by greenhouse gas type for the reporting year (FY2023): CO2 (49,306 metric tons CO2e), CH4 (50 metric tons CO2e), N2O (80 metric tons CO2e), and Other (Refrigerants and R&D) (5,762 metric tons CO2e). All GWP references are from the IPCC Sixth Assessment Report (AR6 - 100 year). [Source: Apple_CDP-Climate-Change-Questionnaire_2024.pdf, Page 108]

Total gross global Scope 1 emissions for the reporting year (FY2023) were 55,200 metric tons CO2e. These emissions are also broken down by business division: Retail Stores (3,104 metric tons CO2e), Corporate (47,235 metric tons CO2e), and Data Centers (4,863 metric tons CO2e). [Source: Apple_CDP-Climate-Change-Questionnaire_2024.pdf, Page 124]

Scope 1 emissions are subject to third-party verification or assurance, with 100% of reported emissions verified under a limited assurance using ISO 14064-3. Apex Companies, LLC conducted this assurance for Apple's fiscal year 2024 reporting period, verifying Scope 1 GHG Emissions at 55,200 metric tonnes of carbon dioxide equivalent (tCO2e) for worldwide occupied properties under the WRI/WBCSD GHG Protocol. [Source: Apple_CDP-Climate-Change-Questionnaire_2024.pdf, Page 101, 102], [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 104]
score_explanation_french: L'entreprise a entièrement quantifié ses émissions de Scope 1 en unités cohérentes (tonnes métriques de CO2e) pour plusieurs exercices fiscaux, y compris une ventilation détaillée par type de gaz à effet de serre et par division d'activité. Les limites de déclaration sont clairement définies selon une approche de contrôle opérationnel, et les méthodologies utilisées sont conformes à la norme d'entreprise du Protocole des Gaz à Effet de Serre (GHG Protocol Corporate Standard). De plus, 100 % des émissions de Scope 1 déclarées ont fait l'objet d'une vérification par un tiers avec une assurance limitée, conformément à la norme ISO 14064-3. Cette divulgation complète remplit tous les critères pour un score de 100.
score_explanation_english: The company has fully quantified its Scope 1 emissions in consistent units (metric tons CO2e) for multiple fiscal years, including a detailed breakdown by greenhouse gas type and business division. The reporting boundaries are clearly defined following an operational control approach, and the methodologies used align with the GHG Protocol Corporate Standard. Furthermore, 100% of the reported Scope 1 emissions have undergone third-party verification with limited assurance, adhering to ISO 14064-3. This comprehensive disclosure fulfills all criteria for a score of 100.

user_friendly_explanation: Apple is very thorough in reporting its indirect greenhouse gas emissions, known as Scope 2 emissions, which come from the energy it buys for its operations. They provide clear numbers in tons of CO2 equivalent for both where the energy is physically used and where it's accounted for financially. This includes all their offices, stores, and data centers globally. They also clearly state how they calculate these emissions and confirm that their data is checked and approved by an independent expert. This means you can trust that Apple knows its Scope 2 emissions inside and out.
information: Apple's carbon footprint boundary is aligned with the Greenhouse Gas (GHG) Protocol framework and includes emissions that are material and relevant to Apple, where data is available. Apple's carbon footprint includes direct scope 1 emissions; indirect scope 2 emissions from purchased electricity, steam, heating, and cooling; and indirect scope 3 emissions from purchased goods and services, other fuel and energy related activities, transportation and distribution, business travel, employee commute, product use, and end of life. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 82]

Apple is carbon neutral for corporate emissions as of April 2020. Beginning in fiscal year 2022, we've expanded our footprint boundary to include scope 3 emissions associated with work from home, other cloud services, electricity transmission and distribution losses, and other fuel and energy-related activities. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 82]
Beginning in fiscal year 2021, we're accounting for scope 2 emissions from the purchase of district heating, chilled water, and steam. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 82]

Apple follows the GHG Protocol Corporate Standard for quantifying and reporting direct greenhouse gas emissions. Apple utilizes actual consumption data sourced from utility bills, invoices, meters data, landlord reports, or vendor reports. In cases where actual data is unavailable, Apple employs estimation factors per square foot per year, derived from historical data and trends from similar facilities (e.g., retail spaces or corporate offices). These estimation factors are then multiplied by the total floor space to estimate the annual consumption. To calculate Scope 2 emissions from the consumption data, Apple applies the appropriate emission factors from utilities emission factors, USA EPA e-grid, International Energy Agency, local governments sets, or residual mix, which are also updated annually. [Source: Apple_CDP-Climate-Change-Questionnaire_2024.pdf, Page 83]

For fiscal year 2024, Scope 2 (market-based) emissions are 3,300 metric tons CO2e, and Scope 2 (location-based) emissions are 1,224,500 metric tons CO2e. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 82; Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 104; Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 105]

For fiscal year 2023, Scope 2 (market-based) emissions are 3,400 metric tons CO2e, and Scope 2 (location-based) emissions are 1,206,700 metric tons CO2e. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2024.pdf, Page 77; Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2024.pdf, Page 97; Apple_CDP-Climate-Change-Questionnaire_2024.pdf, Page 90]

Scope 2 market-based emissions from purchased electricity is zero. But, we also account for purchased steam, heating, and cooling, which resulted in 3,300 metric tons of emissions in fiscal year 2024. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 99]

Scope 2 market-based emissions from purchased electricity is zero. But, we also account for purchased steam, heating, and cooling, which resulted in 3,400 metric tons of emissions in fiscal year 2023. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2024.pdf, Page 88]
Scope 2 emissions (market-based) for Corporate Facilities are 3,400 tCO2e in FY2023. [Source: Apple_CDP-Climate-Change-Questionnaire_2024.pdf, Page 125]

The Scope 2 location-based emissions for FY2023 are broken down by business division: Corporate Facilities (296,599 tCO2e), Retail Stores (629,098 tCO2e), Distribution Centers (19,256 tCO2e), Data centers (186,519 tCO2e), and Co-located data centers (75,264 tCO2e). [Source: Apple_CDP-Climate-Change-Questionnaire_2024.pdf, Page 125]

Scope 2 emissions (market-based) are 0 tCO2e for Retail Stores, Distribution Centers, Data centers, and Co-located data centers in FY2023. [Source: Apple_CDP-Climate-Change-Questionnaire_2024.pdf, Page 125]
The total energy consumption for corporate facilities for 2024 (renewable and non-renewable) is 3,777,000 MWh. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 86]
The total energy consumption for corporate facilities for 2023 (renewable and non-renewable) is 3,487,000 MWh. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2024.pdf, Page 81]

Scope 2 emissions (both location-based and market-based) are verified by a third-party (Apex) to ISO14064-3 standard, with 100% of reported emissions verified. [Source: Apple_CDP-Climate-Change-Questionnaire_2024.pdf, Page 101; Apple_CDP-Climate-Change-Questionnaire_2024.pdf, Page 103]
score_explanation_french: Apple fournit des données d'émissions de Scope 2 entièrement quantifiées dans des unités cohérentes (tCO₂e) pour les approches basées sur l'emplacement et sur le marché, pour l'année de référence (AF2024) et les années précédentes. Le périmètre de déclaration est clairement défini comme 'contrôle opérationnel' et couvre les 'propriétés occupées dans le monde entier', y compris les installations d'entreprise, les centres de données, les magasins de détail et les centres de données co-localisés. L'entreprise rend compte explicitement de tous les composants des émissions de Scope 2, y compris l'électricité, la vapeur, le chauffage et la climatisation achetés. Des méthodologies détaillées conformes à la norme d'entreprise du protocole GHG sont divulguées, y compris l'application de facteurs d'émission spécifiques. De plus, les données d'émissions de Scope 2 sont vérifiées à 100 % par un tiers (Apex) par rapport à la norme ISO 14064-3. La répartition des émissions basées sur le marché et sur l'emplacement par division commerciale et zone géographique est également fournie. Tous ces aspects indiquent une divulgation complète et transparente des émissions de Scope 2.
score_explanation_english: Apple provides fully quantified Scope 2 emissions data in consistent units (tCO₂e) for both location-based and market-based approaches for the reporting year (FY2024) and prior years. The reporting boundary is clearly defined as 'operational control' and covers 'Worldwide occupied properties', including corporate facilities, data centers, retail stores, and co-located data centers. The company explicitly accounts for all components of Scope 2 emissions, including purchased electricity, steam, heating, and cooling. Detailed methodologies adhering to the GHG Protocol Corporate Standard are disclosed, including the application of specific emission factors. Furthermore, the Scope 2 emissions data is 100% verified by a third-party (Apex) against the ISO 14064-3 standard. The breakdown of market-based and location-based emissions by business division and geographical area is also provided. All these aspects indicate a comprehensive and transparent disclosure of Scope 2 emissions.

level_2_metrics.metric_name: Pollution Of Air Water And Soil

level_3_metrics.metric_name: Pollution Emissions To Air Water And Soil
user_friendly_explanation: Apple is doing a strong job in controlling and reducing pollution. The company has clear, measurable goals to cut down harmful emissions from its factories and suppliers, including specific targets for reducing certain industrial gases and for reusing water. They also have a detailed public list of banned or restricted chemicals in their products and manufacturing processes. Apple publicly shares how much pollution they've prevented, for example, the millions of tons of specific gases their suppliers have avoided releasing, the billions of gallons of water they discharge, and the tons of chemical waste they've reduced. They actively manage pollution risks by carefully checking all materials for harmful chemicals, investing in cleaner alternatives, and ensuring proper wastewater and stormwater management. Furthermore, Apple's factories are certified under a strict international environmental management system (ISO 14001), showing a systematic effort to protect the environment.
information: One of the largest contributors of direct emissions in our supply chain is the use of fluorinated greenhouse gases (F-GHGs). F-GHGs are notably used in the electronics manufacturing of semiconductors and flat-panel displays, and their global warming potential (GWP) is orders of magnitude higher than that of CO2. While the use of F-GHGs in certain manufacturing processes today is difficult to avoid, emissions can be reduced by switching to alternative low GWP gases, optimizing production processes to use and emit fewer F-GHGs, and installing gas abatement tools. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 31]
In 2024, our display suppliers' efforts resulted in avoided emissions of more than 8.4 million metric tons CO2e, annualized. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 31]

As of March 2025, 100 percent of our applicable display suppliers and 26 of our semiconductor suppliers with direct manufacturing for Apple have committed to these reductions [at least 90 percent abatement of total facility F-GHG emissions]. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 32]

In 2024, our display and semiconductor suppliers' abatement efforts reduced more than 8.4 million annualized metric tons of CO2e for Apple-related production. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 32]

Water: Our purpose We aim to advance water security and protect communities where we and our suppliers operate through actions that improve freshwater availability, quality, and equity. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 48]

Goal: Increase supplier participation in the Supplier Clean Water Program, prioritizing locations with high water stress and supporting participants in achieving a 50 percent average water reuse rate by 2030. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 49]
We then design solutions to manage the quality of the wastewater that we return to the watershed. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 49]
Our low-water design efforts avoided 161 million gallons of freshwater use in 2024 in our corporate offices and data centers. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 49]

We require our suppliers to maintain the high standards for water discharge outlined in the Apple Supplier Code of Conduct. Through our Supplier Clean Water Program, we help suppliers minimize process water impacts and adopt best practices in water management and wastewater treatment. The program supports our suppliers in going above and beyond requirements to become stewards of the water resources where they operate by conserving water, promoting water reuse, and preventing water pollution within our supply chain. Since the program's launch in 2013, the average reuse rate of more than 250 participating suppliers has increased to 42 percent, saving 14 billion gallons of freshwater in 2024 and over 90 billion gallons of water. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 50]

Water Quality Protection and WASH in Northern Nevada: The project addresses water quality in the river and provides sanitation and water for drinking and hygiene to the downtown river corridor. The installation of a new Portland Loo in Reno City Plaza, along with continued operation and maintenance of freestanding public restrooms along the river in area parks, will prevent bacterial and nutrient pollution while providing access to dignified toilet facilities for the estimated 2000 people experiencing homelessness in Reno. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 51]

We also work to reduce complex waste streams, including chemical waste generated during manufacturing. In 2024, we continued our efforts in coolant recycling and reuse throughout our supply chain... We achieved positive results in 2024, reducing coolant-containing waste by more than 36,000 metric tons through fine filtration and sterilization. We focus on recovering valuable metals from waste liquids generated during printed circuit board (PCB) manufacturing, including copper (Cu), gold (Au), and palladium (Pd). These metals are efficiently separated and processed into high-quality raw materials using advanced extraction and purification technologies. The recovered metals are then reintegrated into the production cycle. In 2024, approximately 1800 metric tons of metals and metal compounds were recovered through this process. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 55]

Smarter Chemistry: We identify and use the chemicals and materials that best serve our priorities of safety, performance, and the environment... reducing the recirculation of potentially harmful substances. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 58]

This Regulated Substances Specification describes Apple's global requirements and restrictions on the use of certain chemical substances or materials in a) Apple products, accessories, and packaging (Section 3) b) ingredient formulations such as adhesives and inks that are applied, cured, or compounded into Apple products, accessories, and packaging (Section 4), and c) manufacturing processes (Section 5). Restrictions are derived from international laws or directives, regulatory agencies, eco-label requirements, environmental standards, and Apple policies. Apple's restrictions may go beyond regulatory requirements in order to protect human health and the environment. [Source: Apple_Regulated_Substances_Specification.pdf, Page 2]

The specification designates restricted substances and requires reporting on additional substances... Most recently, we've added perfluorohexanesulfonic acid (PFHxA) - a subset of the larger group of PFAS - phenol, isopropylated, phosphate (3:1) (PIP 3:1), and several skin-sensitizing substances well ahead of regulatory restrictions. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 61]

In 2024, we performed toxicological assessments on more than 1800 new materials to proactively evaluate and eliminate potentially harmful substances from our products. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 64]

In 2024, we continued providing additional support to suppliers through trainings on the regulations, attended by more than 480 participants. These attendees helped validate over 3900 materials for low-VOC compliance. By deploying a VOC specification worldwide, we're also helping drive adoption of low-VOC alternatives around the world. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 65]

We've eliminated the use of cleaners with known carcinogens, mutagens, reproductive toxicants, strong sensitizers and persistent bio-accumulative toxins (including PFAS) from cleaners and degreasers used at our supplier final assembly sites. In 2024, we approved 67 additional safer cleaners for use in our supply chain, bringing the total number of safer cleaners we've approved over the past three years to more than 200. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2os25.pdf, Page 65]

We've been working to phase out the use of PFAS in our products, engaging with our supply chain partners, and developing alternatives. We've created new formulations of plastics, adhesives, and lubricants, replacing PFAS with other existing technologies to achieve similar performance in flame resistance and friction reduction. We're also advancing the ability to more effectively detect PFAS. We've developed a test method for more accurate measuring fluorine in plastics to better monitor PFAS in consumer electronics. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 68]

In partnership with our suppliers, we've created dye formulations in our anodizing processes that better safeguard worker health and the environment... These alternatives mitigate the risks associated with conventional dyes used in anodizing processes, including potential workplace exposure and impact on the local environment through discharge. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 69]

Environment, Health and Safety Policy... Apple Inc. is committed to protecting the environment, health and safety (EHS) of our employees, customers, and contractors... Apply higher standards to protect human health and the environment where laws and regulations do not provide adequate controls. Promote responsible management and stewardship of clean energy, water, waste, resources, and biodiversity. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 122]

Apple strives for continuous improvement in our EHS management systems and in the environmental quality of our products, processes, and services. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 122]
Apple operates manufacturing facilities in Cork, Ireland. We certify 100 percent of these facilities with ISO 14001. [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 123]
F-GHG Emissions Reduced from Abatement Quantity: 8,407,304 Units: Metric Tons CO2e [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 97]
Water Discharge (Million gallons): 900 [Source: Apple_Environmental_Progress_Report_2025.pdf, Page 87]
iPhone 16e is free of harmful substances like brominated flame retardants, PVC, phthalates, arsenic in glass, and mercury. [Source: iPhone_16e_PER_Feb2025.pdf, Page 1]
Mac Studio is free of harmful substances like brominated flame retardants, PVC, phthalates, arsenic in glass, and mercury. [Source: Mac_Studio_PER_March2025.pdf, Page 4]

Apple Vision Pro is free of harmful substances like beryllium, brominated flame retardants, PVC, phthalates, arsenic in the display glass, and mercury. [Source: Apple_Vision_Pro_PER_Feb2024.pdf, Page 5]

Apple Watch Ultra 2 is free of harmful substances like beryllium, brominated flame retardants, PVC, phthalates, arsenic in the display glass, and mercury. [Source: Apple_Watch_Ultra_2_PER_Sept2024.pdf, Page 5]
Apple Watch SE is free of harmful substances like beryllium, brominated flame retardants, PVC, phthalates, arsenic in the display glass, and mercury. [Source: Apple_Watch_SE_PER_Sept2024.pdf, Page 5]

The Regulated Substances Specification describes Apple's global requirements and restrictions on the use of certain chemical substances or materials... [Source: Apple_Regulated_Substances_Specification.pdf, Page 2]

Section 3.1. Products and Packaging: Restrictions lists threshold limits for various chemical or chemical groups including: Antimony; Arsenic; Asbestos; Azo dyes, arylamines, anilines; Beryllium; Bisphenol A (BPA); Bromine and brominated compounds; Brominated and chlorinated flame retardants; Cadmium; Chlorinated paraffins; Chlorine; Dechlorane Plus; Dimethylfumarate (DMFu); Formaldehyde; Halogenated diphenyl methanes; Heavy metals (Cd + Cr (VI) + Hg + Pb); Hexabromocyclododecane (HBCDD); Hexavalent chromium (Cr (VI)); Lead; Mercury; Natural rubber, latex; Nickel; Organotin compounds; Ortho-phthalates; Perchlorates; Poly- and perfluoroalkyl substances (PFAS); PFBS; PFCAs (C9-C14); PFHxA; PFHxS; PFOA; PFOS; Phenyl, isopropylated phosphate (3 : 1); Polybrominated biphenyls (PBBs); Polybrominated diphenyl ethers (PBDEs); Polychlorinated biphenyl (PCB); Polychlorinated naphthalenes (PCN); Polychlorinated terphenyl (PCT); Polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs); Polyvinyl chloride (PVC); Radioactive substances; REACH Annex XVII; REACH Candidate List of SVHCs; Skin-sensitizing substances; Tetrabromobisphenyl A (TBBA); UV-328. [Source: Apple_Regulated_Substances_Specification.pdf, Page 6-10]

Section 3.2. Products and Packaging: Reportable Substances and Future Restrictions identifies substances like PFAS, EDCs, various flame retardants, plasticizers, solvents, and heavy metals with 'Begin phaseout immediately' or 'Expect future restrictions' requirements. [Source: Apple_Regulated_Substances_Specification.pdf, Page 11-13]

Section 4.1. Ingredient Formulations: Restricted Substances in Products details restrictions for Benzene, Chlorinated organic solvents, Methyl-phenol compounds, Mineral oils, n-Hexane, Toluene, and VOCs. [Source: Apple_Regulated_Substances_Specification.pdf, Page 14]

Section 4.2. Ingredient Formulations: Reportable Substances and Future Restrictions lists additional substances like Brominated organic solvents, Ethylbenzene, Heavy metals, Methanol, N-Ethyl-2-pyrrolidone, N-Methyl-2-pyrrolidone, Solvents, VOCs, and Xylene and its isomers. [Source: Apple_Regulated_Substances_Specification.pdf, Page 15]

Section 5.1. Manufacturing Processes: Restrictions prohibits Benzene, Brominated organic solvents, Chlorinated organic solvents, n-Hexane, N-Methylpyrrolidone (NMP), Ozone-depleting chemicals (ODC), and Toluene for certain applications, and sets VOC limits. [Source: Apple_Regulated_Substances_Specification.pdf, Page 16]

Section 5.2. Manufacturing Processes: Reportable Substances and Future Restrictions includes Ethylbenzene, Formaldehyde, Hydrogen fluoride (HF), Methanol, N-Methylpyrrolidone (NMP), PFAS, Toluene, and Xylene and its isomers. [Source: Apple_Regulated_Substances_Specification.pdf, Page 17]

Section 6.2 Alternatives Assessment Requirements mandates comprehensive chemical hazard assessment for replacements of PFAS, Flame retardants, Plasticizers, and Solvents. [Source: Apple_Regulated_Substances_Specification.pdf, Page 18]

Section 6.3 Safer Alternatives Criteria sets acceptance criteria for Cleaners and alternatives to various pollutant-containing materials. [Source: Apple_Regulated_Substances_Specification.pdf, Page 19]

Section 9. Demonstrating Compliance details required test methods for Arsenic, Beryllium, BBP, Cadmium, DBP, DIBP, Hexavalent Chromium, Lead, Mercury, PBB, PBDE, Bromine, Chlorine, Fluorine, PFOA, PFOS, Mineral oils, UV-328, and heavy metals in dye powder formulations, as well as manufacturing process chemicals like Benzene, Brominated organic solvents, Chlorinated organic solvents, n-Hexane, NMP, and Toluene. [Source: Apple_Regulated_Substances_Specification.pdf, Page 23-24]

Apple products contain the following SVHCs above the disclosure threshold: 1,3-propanesultone, 1,2-dimethoxyethane, Bumetrizole (UV-326), 2-(2H-benzotriazol-2-yl)-4,6-ditertpentylphenol (UV-328), 2-(2H-benzotriazol-2-yl)-4-(1,1,3,3-tetramethylbutyl)phenol (UV-329), Bisphenol A (BPA), diphenyl(2,4,6-trimethylbenzoyl)phosphine oxide (TPO), Lead, Lead titanium zirconium oxide, Potassium perfluorobutane sulfonate (PFBS). [Source: Apple_REACH_SVHC_Disclosure.pdf, Page 1]

No ozone‑depleting chemicals (ODCs) are used in any processes to manufacture product components and materials, or product packaging materials used by Apple, as stipulated by the Montreal Protocol on Substances that Deplete the Ozone Layer. [Source: answers]

Our Code and Standards have specific provisions outlining our expectations for suppliers as they relate to water and wastewater management, stormwater management, and resource consumption management. This also includes provisions related to WASH (water supply, sanitation, and hygiene) for supplier dormitories and facilities. [Source: Apples_Water_Strategy.pdf, Page 8]

Water quality refers to ensuring that water is fit for purpose and clean enough to use. Quality is more nuanced than availability. For example, excess nutrients, salts, dissolved solids, or heavy metal pollution can all lower water quality. [Source: Apples_Water_Strategy.pdf, Page 4]

We require our suppliers to identify and assess a broad range of risks, including social, environmental, and human rights risks for materials used in Apple products. [Source: apple-how-we-source-materials-responsibly]

Environmental restoration and community investment: Since 2017, we've worked with the nonprofit RESOLVE on projects aimed at restoring and rehabilitating ecosystems that have been affected by legacy mining operations.
